title: epipactis palustris f. purpurea
intro: Epipactis palustris f. purpurea, also known as the marsh helleborine or purple marsh helleborine, is a unique flowering orchid species that thrives in wetlands and damp environments. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been utilized in traditional herbal remedies for centuries. The roots and leaves of Epipactis palustris f. purpurea have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Epipactis palustris f. purpurea often include infusions, tinctures, and teas, which are made by combining the plant's roots and leaves with water or other solvents to create a medicinal liquid.
